title: abelmoschus manihot var. tetraphyllus
intro: Abelmoschus manihot var. tetraphyllus, also known as the West Indian manjack or four-leaf hibiscus, is a perennial plant species of the mallow family, characterized by its large, showy flowers and medicinal properties. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds, which contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-diabetic, and anti-cancer properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Abelmoschus manihot var. tetraphyllus is prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, to utilize its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
